news.headline: Sonny Rollins, legendary saxophone colossus, dead at 95
news.summary:
Saxophonist Sonny Rollins, who spent more than two years practising in solitude as a young man on a windswept New York bridge to reinvent his playing and ⁠become one of the giants of jazz, died at the ⁠age of 95 on Monday, his publicist said.
Rollins had recorded the confidently titled Jazz Colossus

news.headline: Istanbul’s Bilgi University reopened after police crackdown
news.summary: Students celebrated after days of protests against the closure decree that would have shut Bilgi University mid-year.

news.headline: Cost of living crisis reshapes Eid spending in Nigeria
news.summary: High costs in Nigeria are reshaping Eid preparations as families adjust spending and cut back on celebrations.

news.headline: SC rules against khula sans wife’s explicit consent
news.summary: <p>ISLAMABAD: The Supreme Court has ruled that family courts should not convert a wife’s suit for dissolution of marriage on grounds of cruelty into a decree of khula without her explicit and informed consent, particularly when valuable financial rights such as unpaid dower are involved.</p

news.headline: Iran national football team to stay in Mexico during 2026 World Cup
news.summary: Iran's national football team will base itself in Mexico during the 2026 FIFA World Cup and travel into the United States only on matchdays. news.headline: Is the US unsafe and unwelcoming? Trump’s Australia-born tourism chief addresses ‘myths’
news.summary:
“America is open, America is ready for you. We want you to come”.
The United States’ new tourism chief, Nick Adams, boldly delivered the invitation at last week’s IPW Fort Lauderdale inbound travel trade show in Florida.
“We can protect our borders and open our doors at the same time,” he insisted.

news.headline: U.S. Carries Out Renewed Strikes in Southern Iran
news.summary: Military officials said that the strikes targeted missile sites near a major Iranian port that threatened U.S. ships.

news.headline: SHC acquits ex-NBP chief in Rs54bn loan scam case
news.summary: <p>KARACHI: The Sindh High Court (SHC) on Monday acquitted former National Bank of Pakistan (NBP) president Syed Ahmed Iqbal Ashraf in the Rs54 billion Hascol loan scam case, observing that nearly all other accused persons had been exonerated, acquitted or converted into witnesses. </p>

news.headline: China Mobile plans fresh Central Asia link to Hong Kong submarine cable network
news.summary:
Telecoms giant China Mobile International is planning another route to connect terrestrial cables in Central Asia with Hong Kong’s submarine network, aiming to enable more efficient data transmission between the “stan” countries and the rest of the world, a senior executive has said.

news.headline: How a Curious FIFA Deal Gave Fox a Huge Bargain for World Cup Broadcast Rights
news.summary: A decision to stave off litigation between Fox and FIFA turned into a bonanza worth hundreds of millions of dollars in discounted World Cup rights to the broadcaster.

news.headline: Rescuers race to free seven people trapped in flooded Laos cave
news.summary: A team of experts who helped free a teen football team from a Thai cave in 2018 are among the rescuers.

news.headline: Middle East live: US targets Iranian vessels, missile sites in new strikes
news.summary: US forces struck missile sites and vessels allegedly laying naval mines in southern Iran late on Monday, the United States Central Command said. news.headline: Starbucks Korea’s ‘Tank Day’ blunder sparks personal apology from Shinsegae boss
news.summary:
Shinsegae Group chairman Chung Yong-jin on Tuesday apologised to the public in person, eight days after Starbucks Korea, run by the conglomerate, sparked controversy with its “Tank Day” marketing event, which critics say inappropriately referenced South Korea’s pro-democracy movement of the 1980s.
